OPERATOR MODULE REV. 2.9 (PRO)

PROFESSIONAL DUAL-DECK
DJ MIX STATION

Complete structural operators handbook. Learn the mechanical layout, system integration pathways, and professional mixing physics of your browser workstation.

● SYSTEM CONNECTED
AUDIO CORE:WEB AUDIO API
SAMPLE RATE:48,000 HZ / STEREO
DECKS ACTIVE:A [CYAN] | B [PURPLE]
LATENCY DEPTH:< 15 MS (OPTIMIZED)
Handbook Index
01 // Welcome Ceremony 02 // The Decks (A & B) 03 // JogWheels & Scratching 04 // Cues & Hot Points 05 // Channel Mixer Console 06 // FX Engine & Rolls 07 // Sound Libraries 08 // Step-By-Step Mix
TACTILE ALERT

Most controls are real knobs & sliders. Click & drag them upward or downward to pivot, twist, or slide. Never click once and assume!

SECTION 01

THE PHILOSOPHY OF SOUND MANIPULATION

Welcome to your professional audio station. This application is designed to mimic a high-end physical hardware DJ controller setup (such as Pioneer DJ Nexus systems or Native Instruments Kontrol).

As a DJ (Disc Jockey), your primary objective is seamless continuity: blending two tracks back-to-back so flawlessly that the audience feels as though they are experiencing a single, endless, evolving musical journey. To do this, you must control three core pillars of audio: Tempo (speed in beats-per-minute), Timbre (frequency EQ and filters), and Amplitude (volume mixing). This manual maps every instrument at your fingertips to those pillars.

01 // SELECT CLARITY

Choose system music in the crate at the bottom, load on Deck A/B, and watch waveforms parse in real time.

02 // MATCH VELOCITIES

Use pitch/tempo faders or hit the SYNC button to align the beats of both channels instantly.

03 // BLEND SUB-FREQUENCIES

Cut clashing basslines using EQs while crossfading gently to complete the transitional magic.

SECTION 02

THE CHANNEL DECKS (A & B)

The console split is symmetrical. DECK A (left, highlighted in Cyan) and DECK B (right, highlighted in Purple) represent your isolated music players. Each player behaves like an ultra-smart, touch-sensitive vinyl turntable.

CYAN = TRACK A // PURPLE = TRACK B

Deck Control Interface Block

Transport Area

Controls playback triggers, directions, and instant beat-seeking speeds.

REW START PLAY / PAUSE REV DIRECTION
<< FEW SECS CUE POINT FEW SECS >>
Velocity Engine

Controls playhead frequency, aligns speeds, and locks key frequencies.

Key Lock (MT): Preserves vocals
Tempo Fader: ±50% Range
Sync Trigger: Auto-RPM Match
Output Trim

Balances structural audio gain prior to entering the master mixing console.

GAIN KNOB 0% to 200% Vol
Control Label Action Form Action Outcome & DJ Purpose
PLAY / PAUSE Single Click / Touch Triggers playback engine at set BPM. Click again to pause audio immediately. Pausing stops the rotating vinyl visual in the center platter.
FAST FORWARD / REWIND Single Click Circular buttons with forward (>>) and rewind (<<) icons beside the CUE trigger. Instantly jumps the track timeline 10 seconds forward or backward, perfect for clean navigation and searching beats.
CUE Press & Hold, or Tap If song is playing: taps return playhead directly to the orange CUE point and pause. If song is paused: holding CUE temporarily previews the track from the start. Letting go instantly snaps back to the mark. Always use this to check beats!
REWIND Single Click Instantly returns playhead time to exactly 00:00 (absolute track start). Exceptional for restarting tracks during failures or starting fresh.
REV (Reverse) Click to Toggle On/Off Inverts play direction. Let the track spin completely backwards. When active, it glows amber (Deck A) or pink (Deck B). Crucial for creating psychedelic beat builds before drops.
MT (Master Tempo) Click to Toggle On/Off Toggles "Key Lock". When locked, you can change track speed without changing its musical key. Vocals will sound crystal clear even if played 20% faster, instead of sounding like chipmunks.
SYNC Single Click Instantly matches the play rate (BPM) of this deck's track to the BPM of the opposite deck. Perfect for eliminating mechanical manual pitch matching error.
GAIN Click & Drag Up / Down Pre-fader preamp signal volume. Amplifies or attenuates individual songs up to 2.0x (200%). If your loaded local file is too quiet, twist this up to balance it with Track B.
TEMPO Slider Click & Drag Vertical Physical speed fader. Adjusts track speed permanently up to ±50%. Moving the slider DOWN speeds the song up (traditional Pioneer standard). Moving UP slows it down.
SECTION 03

THE JOOWHEELS & THE ART OF SCRATCHING

The giant circular discs in the center of both decks are touch-sensitive JogWheels / Platters. These are not merely spinning graphics; they possess fully simulated physical weight, surface drag, and multi-touch sensors.

PRO MOTION DECK SENSING

When a track plays, the outer ring is styled like strobe-dots rotating in perfect synchronicity. You can see your live track's play-position indicated by the glowing color markers (Cyan or Purple).

The center display isolates track BPM calculations and shows your exact temporary pitch bend values as a active percentage.

PHYSICAL OPERATOR GESTURES

  • Left-Click & Drag (Circular): Grabs the vinyl surface. Dragging back-and-forth simulates real vinyl scratching, injecting live mechanical vinyl sound waves and letting you scrub perfectly to a beat.
  • Vertical Scroll Drag: Triggers temporary deceleration or acceleration (Pitch Bend) up to ±8%. Release the grab to snap back to the exact grid tempo instantly.
PRO-TRAINING LAB #1: SCRATCH TESTING

Load an active track on Deck A. Hit play to get it spinning. Click and hold the center of the outer platter and slide it in quick horizontal or clock-wise arcs. You will hear a realistic pitch-bent vector scratching sound, and track playhead will lock onto your cursor instantly. Let go, and the track will instantly resume playing normally from that spot.

SECTION 04

CUES & THE "1-2-3-4" HOT POINTS

A fundamental difference between playheads and professional DJ interfaces is CUE management. Instead of scrubbing an entire track to find a spot, clean DJs mark instant target points.

1. MASTER ORANGE CUE POINT

When a song is paused, the location you are on becomes the default base point. This spot is marked in the central wave spectrum with an Orange Vertical line labeled "CUE".

Tapping the big round CUE button will instantly snap your playhead exactly to this orange marker and pause. This is your anchor – if your mix clashes, drop the cue to start again.

2. THE 1, 2, 3, 4 HOT CUE QUADRANT

Underneath each deck, on the right side of the control block, is a grid of 4 buttons numbered 1, 2, 3, 4. These are your Hot Cues:

  • By default, these squares are dim and display empty values.
  • While a song is playing, click any empty pad (e.g., 1). It will capture the exact microsecond and begin to glow in Deck A's color.
  • Now, at any point in the set (even if playing or paused), clicking that glowing pad will instantly warp you to that exact spot without pausing!
  • To clear these, click the red "RESET" button located above the pads.
PRO-TRAINING LAB #2: HOT CUE WARPING

Play a track on Deck A. Right before the bass drop, click pad 1. Note that the pad lights up cyan. Let the song play for another 30 seconds. Now click pad 1. Watch how playback instantly warps back to the bass drop seamlessly. Also notice the spectacular red marker labeled "HC1" printed directly on the waveform spectrum!

SECTION 05

THE CENTRAL MIXER CONSOLE

The center console acts as your structural blending matrix. The console accepts audio from Deck A and Deck B, shapes their resonant frequencies, and merges them to the master stereo speaker outputs.

1. FREQUENCY ISOLATORS

Three rotary dials per channel control precise frequency isolators:

  • • HI: Adjusts highs (hiss, hats, air).
  • • MID: Adjusts mids (voice, synths).
  • • LOW: Boosts or cuts sub-bass kicks.

⚠️ NEVER blend two sub-basses at 100%. Cut one bass to 0% to avoid muddy, distorted speaker clipping!

2. BIPOLAR VOICE FILTERS

The FILTER dial cuts high and low profiles at once:

  • • LEFT (Negative): Low-pass filter (LPF). Cuts all highs; track sounds muffled (underwater effect).
  • • RIGHT (Positive): High-pass filter (HPF). Cuts all sub-line bass; track sounds thin.

Double-tap or drag to the pure 0 (center) to disable filters entirely.

3. LINE FADERS & CURVES

The linear vertical sliders control channel levels, feeding into the horizontal crossfader bottom matrix:

  • • CURVE: Tightens crossfader fade.
  • • CLEAN SOUND: Resets Web Audio buffers instantly during glitch/stutters.
  • • RESET CONSOLE: Instant zero-points.

The central LED strip maps live amplitude volume level limits with green/yellow/red bulbs.

SECTION 06

THE SPECIALIZED FX ENGINE & BEAT ROLLS

Want to turn standard vocals into warehouse stadium spectacles? Tapping the small arrow button (Chevron) on the side of the Jogwheels rotates the controls to expose the Specialized FX Engine Panel.

FOUR TACTILE FX ISOLATORS

● ECHO:

Creates repeating delays that slowly break down. Great for fading out tracks on transition sections.

● FLANGER (FLAN):

Takes slightly duplicated phase voice tracks to simulate a sweeping wind, jet-engine, or robotic whoosh effect.

● BITCRUSHER (BIT):

Reduces sampling resolution rate artificially. Gritty 8-bit digital sound, spectacular for industrial synth segments.

● REVERB (VERB):

Creates spatial cavernous delay. Floods vocals into high-end wet stadium reverberations.

NON-SLIP BEAT ROLLS (STUTTER BLOCKS)

Located above the rotary dials is a bar containing 1/4, 1/8, and 1/16 Beat Rolls:

  • Press and hold down any pad (e.g. 1/8) during playback.
  • The audio engine will capture that tiny length of track and loop it continuously (stutter effect).
  • Crucially, the timeline keeps running in the background! When you let go, playback snaps perfectly back onto the live grid.
💾 INTEGRATED SET REBOOT

Click "SAVE FX PRESET TO TRACK" to save these custom FX knob positions. Next time you load this track, your custom dials recreate themselves automatically!

SECTION 07

THE LIBRARY CRATES & DIGITAL INTAKE

At the bottom of your workstation lies your musical record case. You can leverage four dedicated intake streams to prep your set:

1. SYSTEM FAVS Contains royalty-free organic tracks from Pixabay. Ready to mix out-of-the-box.
2. AUDIUS NETWORK Search and stream 1 million+ underground techno and house tracks instantly via cloud ingestion API.
3. YOUTUBE / STREAM Enter any stream URL and tap Load. This uses Web Player pipelines directly inside your canvas!
4. LOCAL FILE DROP Drag any raw `.mp3` or `.wav` from your laptop or PC straight onto the console.
SECTION 08

THE DJ MASTERCLASS: PERFORMING YOUR FIRST MIX

Now that you've surveyed every dial, let us put your theory to the test. Follow these exact steps to transition from Track A to Track B without clashing.

1
Configure Deck A live on air

Load a track onto Deck A. Push Channel A Volume (CH A) Slider up to 100%. Move the horizontal crossfader all the way to the far LEFT (Deck A). Tap Play on Deck A. Check that the spectrum is jumping and you can hear the track playing loud and clear.

2
Prepare Deck B in Silent Isolation

Load your second track onto Deck B. Pull CH B Volume Slider down to exactly 0% (Silent).

3
Synchronize tempos

Look at Deck B's BPM display. Tap the SYNC button on Deck B. Its BPM display will change to match Deck A's speed. Now their beats are perfectly aligned in the software.

4
Set your sub-EQ barriers

On the mixer channel for B, twist the LOW EQ dial and the FILTER knobs counterclockwise to cut the volume of the heavy bass beats. DJs never layer two kicks!

5
Trigger Deck B on the Beat

Watch Deck A's bar progression. On the first beat of a new musical phrase (usually on drum snaps or vocal starts, every 16 or 32 counts), hit PLAY on Deck B. Use the CUE button or hot cue 1 to quickly go back and retry if your hand is late!

6
Perform the Frequency Swap

Slowly push CH B fader to 100%. The crowd can now hear Track B's high hi-hats and vocals, but no heavy clashing drum. Now, move the crossfader to the exact middle. At the perfect section change: Quickly turn Track A's LOW knob to minimum, while simultaneously restoring Track B's LOW knob to the center! The energy has just completely shifted to Track B while preserving the groove.

7
Fade out Track A

Gradually pull CH A fader down to 0% volume, slide the crossfader all the way to the far RIGHT, and hit pause on Deck A. You have just successfully completed your first transition. Prep your next track on Deck A and keep the party rocking!

PRO-GRADE DUAL DECK DJ CONSOLE // INSTRUCTION & CALIBRATION REFERENCE FILE

Created with precision and hardware integrity. Review and test every dial directly on the live dashboard.
